Specifically, under the "Epic Games" section, it details a lawsuit filed by Epic Games, Inc. in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California ("California District Court"). This lawsuit alleges violations of federal and state antitrust laws and California's unfair competition law based upon the Company's operation of its App Store.  The California District Court issued the "2021 Injunction," which prevents the Company from prohibiting developers from including in their apps buttons, external links, or other calls to action that direct customers to purchasing mechanisms other than the Company’s in-app purchase system. This injunction applies to apps on the U.S. storefronts of the iOS and iPadOS App Stores.  On April 30, 2025, the California District Court found the Company to be in violation of the 2021 Injunction and enjoined the Company from imposing any commission or fee on purchases made outside an app, restricting how developers guide consumers to outside purchases, or otherwise interfering with a consumer’s choice to proceed in or out of an app. The Company appealed this decision to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit. The Company's request to stay the decision pending appeal was denied, but the Ninth Circuit Court agreed to consider the appeal on an expedited basis, with oral arguments heard in October 2025, as stated in the 2025 Form 10-K.
